## Ready-to-Cite Claims

Drop these into a blog verbatim — they are pre-computed from the live corpus and are safe to quote. Re-run this generator before citing to ensure freshness.

- Across 294 published personality profiles on 9takes, Enneagram types are not evenly distributed — Type 3 (Achiever) is the most common at 19.7% of the corpus.
- Among 103 profiles in the Film & TV category on 9takes, Type 2 (Helper) is over-represented at 13.6% — +5.43 percentage points above the corpus baseline.
- Among 68 profiles in the Creators & Internet Personalities category on 9takes, Type 3 (Achiever) is over-represented at 29.4% — +9.68 percentage points above the corpus baseline.
- Among 50 profiles in the Musicians & Artists category on 9takes, Type 4 (Individualist) is over-represented at 38.0% — +24.39 percentage points above the corpus baseline.
- Among 45 profiles in the Politics & Public Figures category on 9takes, Type 2 (Helper) is over-represented at 15.6% — +7.39 percentage points above the corpus baseline.
- Among 44 profiles in the Tech, Founders & Business category on 9takes, Type 5 (Investigator) is over-represented at 20.4% — +13.99 percentage points above the corpus baseline.
- Among 23 profiles in the Comedians category on 9takes, Type 7 (Enthusiast) is over-represented at 39.1% — +24.84 percentage points above the corpus baseline.
- Among 10 profiles in the Authors & Thinkers category on 9takes, Type 5 (Investigator) is over-represented at 30.0% — +23.54 percentage points above the corpus baseline.
- 101 additional profiles are in the review pipeline, with ~24.3 new profiles shipping per month.

---

## Methodology

- **Source:** 9takes public-figure corpus. One row per profiled person.
- **Scope of public-facing stats:** published profiles only. Unpublished drafts are excluded from all percentages and over/under-representation deltas.
- **Domain buckets:** Raw `type` labels are grouped into readable categories (e.g. `movieStar` + `newMovieStar` + `actor` → Actors). Domains with fewer than 10 profiled figures are omitted to avoid small-sample noise.
- **Over/under-representation:** Each domain's type share minus the corpus-wide baseline share, in percentage points. Positive means over-represented vs. the 9takes corpus average, not vs. general population.
- **Multi-domain figures:** A person tagged with both `musician` and `activist` is counted in both domains.

_Regenerate with `pnpm gen:corpus-stats`. Refresh cadence: monthly._
